titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jeremy Reed Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 19 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 5, 2025 at 23:00
Updated by Rosemary K. J. Davis to add recent acquisitions.
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 21-22, 30, and 43-45, 61 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 46 and 63 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from R. A. Gekoski Booksellers on the George Henry Nettleton Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1998-2007.
May 2010 acquisition: Purchased from Jeremy Reed on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2010.
July 2013 acquisition: Purchased from Jeremy Reed on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.
December 2015 acquisition: Purchased from Thomas A. Glasswater Rare Books, Inc. on James Osborn and Marie Louis Fund, 2015.
July 2021 acquisition: Purchased from Jeremy Reed on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are organized into fifteen groupings: October 1998 Acquisition, November 1999 Acquisition, September 2002 Acquisition, May 2007 Acquisition, August 2007 Acquisition, May 2010 Acquisition, July 2013 acquisition, December 2015 acquisition, July 2016 acquisition, June 2017 acquisition, August 2017 acquisition, March 2019 acquisition, July 2019 acquisition, July 2021 acquisition [1 of 2], and July 2021 acquisition [2 of 2].

bioghist.head: Jeremy Reed, 1951-
bioghist.p: Jeremy Reed, British author, was born March 6, 1951 in Jersey, Channel Islands. A prolific poet, novelist and translator, Reed has authored dozens of volumes of poetry, prose and cultural analysis.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The Jeremy Reed Papers include materials formerly classed as Uncat MS Vault 871, Uncat MSS 363, Uncat MSS 964, and Uncat MS Vault 825.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Portions of this finding aid are compiled from individual preliminary lists that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2009, which has since been updated to reflect more recent acquisitions.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jeremy Reed Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Jeremy Reed Papers contain writings--including poetry, prose and translations--along with drafts, notebooks, correspondence, and other papers related to the life and work of Jeremy Reed.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jeremy Reed Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Formerly classed as Uncat MS Vault 825.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from R. A. Gekoski Booksellers on the George Henry Nettleton Fund, 1998.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosures include a broadside of the poem "Rondel for the Fourth Decade" and two photographs of David Gascoyne.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Formerly classed as Uncat MS Vault 871.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from R. A. Gekoski Booksellers on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1999.

did.unittitle: Waiting for the Man: A Biography of Lou Reed , typescript and editorial correspondence
Heartbreak Hotel: A novel about Elvis Presley in four volumes , draft, notebooks, holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume 4 is Titled "The Sun King"

did.unittitle: Brian Jones: The Last Decadent , draft, holograph
Chasing Black Rainbows , typescript and editorial correspondence
The Pleasure Chateau , typescript and contractual agreement
Bitter Blue , typescript and manuscript, and editorial correspondence
Prairies of Fever (translation from Arabic to English), hologrpah and typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by two notebooks containing translations of Arabic poets

did.unittitle: Madness: The Price of Poetry , typescript and two corrected drafts
Isidore , draft and revised copies with typescript
Red Eclipse , typescript with two drafts
The Conglomerate: Warhol/Bowie/Ballard , typescript, second draft and editorial correspondence
Delirium: An Appreciation of Arthur Rimbaud , typescript and editorial correspondence
Novalis: Hymns to the Night (translation from German to English), typescript and editorial correspondence
Volcano Smoke at Diamond Beach , holograph (copy), typescript and correspondence
The Coastguard's House , typescript and correspondence
When the Whip Comes Down : A novel about the Marquis De Sade, typescript
Marc Almond at the Palladium , Mark Almond and Scott Walker , and translation from Lautréamont's Maldoror , holograph
Essays on Montale and Scott Walker, holograph, and correspondence
The Pleasure Chateau , Screenplay based on the novel, holograph
Jean Genet translations, holograph and typescript
Translations of Jean Genet and Arabic poems, holograph
Sweet Sister Lyric , typescript and correspondence

Essay on Marc Almond, holograph and printout
Inferno , translations into English, holograph and printout

"Nothing but a star," holograph, and foreword by J. G. Ballard, printout
Jean Genet translations, printouts and correspondence

Pretty Boy , printout, contract and correspondence
"It had to be you" (essay on John Wieners), holograph, typescript and correspondence
Adored and explored , holograph, printout and photographs of Marc Almond
"As Your Eyes are Blue: Lee Harwood's Collected Poems," proof

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintains order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, drafts, photocopies, and other personal papers related to Jeremy Reed's life and work.
Most folder titles are directly transcribed from original folders labeled by Jeremy Reed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, sound recordings, and related materials created by or related to Jeremy Reed's life and work.
Most folder titles are directly transcribed from original folders labeled by Jeremy Reed.

scopecontent.p: These files contain notebooks and reference material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain notebooks volumes 1-7.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains an inscribed typescript and a funeral program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains correspondence and printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains photographs of Frank O'Hara.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains photographs of Francis Bacon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a photograph of Francis Bacon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a painting and correspondence.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is closed to research until January 1, 2050.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: David Gascoyne interview Phoebe Heskath Phoebe Hesketh
NBB. Poet Donol'? off Ja? Wa?This side Davido David Gascoyne Steve Ross interview do not rub off

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Malachi Oliver on the James Marshalland Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2021.

did.unittitle: Manuscript notebook, "Wasn't it a strange way down, Peter Perrett and the Only Ones, intro to the reissue of The one and only Peter Perrett - Homme Fatale, Jeremy Reed, star poet"
